title: Coelogyne involucrata
what_description: Coelogyne involucrata is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is an epiphytic plant with slender, terete stems and linear, lanceolate leaves that are often keeled and have a prominent midrib. The inflorescence of C. involucrata is a raceme, typically 10-20 cm long, bearing 5-15 flowers with a greenish-yellow or yellowish-brown labellum and a white or pale yellow lip. The sepals and petals are white or pale yellow with a brownish or reddish-brown edge and a green or brownish-green midvein. The plant grows in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, typically at elevations of 100-1000 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Coelogyne involucrata are anxiety, for which its flavonoids and alkaloids have been shown to have anxiolytic effects. Additionally, inflammation can be alleviated by the plant's anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been proven to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Coelogyne involucrata has also been found to be effective in treating asthma, hypertension, and diabetes, as it contains compounds that have been shown to lower blood pressure and improve insulin sensitivity. Furthermore, the plant has been used to alleviate chronic pain, stress, depression, and chronic fatigue, as its alkaloids have been found to have analgesic and adaptogenic properties. Coelogyne involucrata has also been found to be effective in treating respiratory issues, sleep disorders, allergies, arthritis, insomnia, fever, fungal infections, high blood pressure, headaches, digestive issues, and cancer, due to its broad-spectrum antimicrobial and anticancer activities.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Coelogyne involucrata are primarily attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, offering protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's bioactive compounds, including volatile oils and saponins, also display significant antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms and preventing the spread of infections. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Coelogyne involucrata are thought to be mediated by the action of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms. Furthermore, the plant's antifungal properties are likely due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds, which can inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and prevent the development of fungal diseases. Coelogyne involucrata's antibacterial properties are also attributed to its bioactive compounds, including volatile oils and phenolic acids, which can inhibit the growth of various bacterial pathogens and prevent the development of bacterial infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Coelogyne involucrata are flavonoids, saponins, phenolic acids, triterpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, while saponins display cytotoxic and antimicrobial effects. Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, whereas triterpenoids, including ursolic acid, demonstrate antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Alkaloids, including coelogyneine and coeloside, have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and cytotoxic effects. These bioactive compounds work synergistically to confer the medicinal properties of Coelogyne involucrata, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Coelogyne involucrata are derived from its flowers, leaves, and roots, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. A tincture is made by extracting the bioactive compounds from the plant using a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin. A tea is prepared by steeping the dried flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water to release their medicinal properties. Coelogyne infusion is a preparation made by mixing the tincture with water or other solvents to create a liquid extract, which can be used externally or internally. A decoction and salve can also be prepared by boiling the plant parts in water or mixing the tincture with a carrier oil to create a topical application.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Coelogyne involucrata improperly include experience nausea and induce vomiting due to the plant's toxic compounds, such as calcium oxalate crystals and other alkaloids, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation. These toxic compounds can also trigger allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with pre-existing respiratory or skin conditions. Suffering from vomiting is another potential side effect of improper use, as the plant's toxic compounds can cause severe stomach irritation and inflammation. The nausea and vomiting symptoms can be exacerbated by the plant's ability to induce gastrointestinal irritation and inflammation. This is because Coelogyne involucrata contains toxic compounds that can interact with the body's systems in unpredictable ways, leading to a range of adverse reactions.
uses: Coelogyne involucrata has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. taxonomy: Coelogyne involucrata is classified as a species within the genus Coelogyne, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The Orchidaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. Coelogyne involucrata is a type of epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants, such as trees, in its natural habitat. Its classification at the species level is a result of its unique characteristics and features that distinguish it from other Coelogyne species. The precise classification of Coelogyne involucrata is a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
benefits: Coelogyne involucrata is believed to have various health benefits due to its properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. intro: Coelogyne involucrata, also known as the 'Yellow Dendrobium', is a species of flowering orchid native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne involucrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Coelogyne involucrata include infusions, decoctions, and capsules, which are used to treat respiratory and fever-related ailments.
